finding.title: IBM DB2 Security Update for Log4j (Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228) found on ip-172-31-20-181

event.description: Vulnerability IBM DB2 Security Update for Log4j (Log4Shell CVE-2021-44228) was found on ip-172-31-20-181
finding.description:
Package	Installed Version	Required Version
log4j-core	2.14.1	2.17.1
vulnerability.title: IBM DB2 Security Update for Log4j
vulnerability.description:
IBM DB2 is vulnerable to remote code execution due to the bundled Apache Log4j library (Log4Shell). Apache Log4j2 versions 2.0-beta9 through 2.14.1 allow JNDI injection leading to arbitrary code execution when a specially crafted string is logged.

Affected Versions:
IBM DB2 using Log4j 2.x before 2.17.1

QID Detection Logic: Authenticated
QID checks for the presence of vulnerable Log4j 2.x jar files bundled with IBM DB2.
vulnerability.remediation.description:
Customers are advised to apply IBM DB2 security bulletin and upgrade Log4j to 2.17.1 or later.

Patch:
IBM DB2 Security Bulletin Log4Shell

event.name: Vulnerability finding event
product.name: Tenable Vulnerability Management
finding.title: TLS Version 1.0 Protocol Detection detected in ec2-3-95-43-239.compute-1.amazonaws.com
finding.description: TLSv1 is enabled and the server supports at least one cipher.
vulnerability.title: TLS Version 1.0 Protocol Detection
vulnerability.description:
The remote service accepts connections encrypted using TLS 1.0. TLS 1.0 has a number of cryptographic design flaws. Modern implementations of TLS 1.0 mitigate these problems, but newer versions of TLS like 1.2 and 1.3 are designed against these flaws and should be used whenever possible.

As of March 31, 2020, Endpoints that aren’t enabled for TLS 1.2 and higher will no longer function properly with major web browsers and major vendors.

PCI DSS v3.2 requires that TLS 1.0 be disabled entirely by June 30, 2018, except for POS POI terminals (and the SSL/TLS termination points to which they connect) that can be verified as not being susceptible to any known exploits.
vulnerability.remediation.description: Enable support for TLS 1.2 and 1.3, and disable support for TLS 1.0.
